name: internal-scanner-ips
description: Suppress alerts from known internal vulnerability scanner IPs (Nessus, Qualys, Rapid7)

name: internal-rfc1918-ranges
description: Reduce noise from alerts where BOTH source and destination are internal RFC1918 — review manually, but lower priority

name: known-safe-domains
description: Trusted cloud/CDN domains unlikely to represent real threats in normal context

name: scheduled-scan-alerts
description: Suppress vulnerability scan completion notifications — handled by vuln management process

name: low-severity-threshold
description: Suppress Info-level alerts automatically — they should go to log review, not triage queue

name: scanner-service-accounts
description: Auth failures from known scanner service accounts are expected noise

name: high-frequency-brute-force-internal
description: AUTH-FAIL-BURST rule is noisy — suppress when fired more than 5 times per run (likely scanner traffic)

name: windows-defender-av-detections
description: Generic Defender AV detections below high severity are handled by endpoint team

name: patch-management-noise
description: Alerts generated by patch management systems during maintenance windows

name: known-safe-backup-processes
description: Backup agents often trigger false positive file-access alerts
